steps.title: Connect goal to reaction
steps.content: Grimes wanted One Last Word to honor important Black poets through the golden shovel method, which uses words from an existing poem as line endings in a new poem. The text notes that this method can be challenging and might create awkward poems, so the reviewers' praise matters because it shows how the finished collection was received.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported conclusions
steps.content: Choice A contradicts the passage because reviewers called the book a beautiful and powerful tribute, showing they understood its purpose. Choice C speculates about Grimes's favorite poets; the passage only says she chose lines by poets such as Claude McKay and Gwendolyn Bennett. Choice D invents an effect on other writers that the text never mentions. The reaction shows that the difficult method successfully produced the tribute Grimes intended. The answer is B.
